page: Microsoft Azure NV72ads_A10_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is configured with 72 dedicated vCPUs and 880.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.22 GB per core. It integrates two NVIDIA A10 GPUs with a combined VRAM capacity of 45 GB, delivering hardware acceleration for parallel computing tasks.

For storage-intensive operations, the server is equipped with 2949 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware configuration provides a resource-dense profile designed to handle massive datasets and intensive graphical computations without relying on external storage bottlenecks.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this server is defined by its high density of memory and GPU resources relative to the CPU core count. This makes it a specialized option for workloads that require substantial memory footprints alongside hardware acceleration. Appropriate workloads include virtual desktop infrastructure, 3D visualization, data science, and large-scale database management.
description: Microsoft Azure NV72ads_A10_v5 is an x86_64 server featuring 72 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 880.0 GB of system memory, and two NVIDIA A10 GPUs with a total of 45 GB VRAM. Equipped with 2949 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capabilities, this Hyper-V virtualized instance is optimized for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated workloads such as virtual desktop infrastructure, data science, and database management.

tagline: A high-memory, dual-GPU accelerated server with dedicated AMD processors and local storage for demanding parallel processing workloads.
